title: Explain the Basic Tipper Tote Handling Sequence
summary: Source-specific explanatory runbook describing the normal tipper tote handling sequence documented in the manual: the tipper receives a tote from an AGV, the gripper senses the tote, the gripper pulls the tote up the tipper, and the tote is emptied into a chute.
when_to_use: Use when an operator or reviewer needs to describe or visually verify the documented normal tipper operating concept from AGV tote receipt through parcel discharge into the chute.
not_for: Recovery from sequence failures or jams
Detailed startup, shutdown, jogging, or maintenance actions
Any procedure requiring controls, commands, timing, or acceptance thresholds not provided in this source section

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe or describe the documented tipper sequence using the manual text and referenced figure.

safety_notes: This candidate is based on a high-level operating description only.
Do not add recovery or troubleshooting actions because the source section provides operating description only.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to a tipper in operation or to the documented operating description

access_required: Access to the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual section 3.2.3 Tippers or equivalent packet evidence
Access to Figure 3-4 for gripper identification if visual component confirmation is needed
system_preconditions: A tipper is available for observation if performing a live visual verification of the documented sequence

visual_references.description: Figure 3-4 identifying tipper components, including the gripper referenced in the operating description.
Operator station reference image showing the tipper and chute area in overall context.

steps.title: Confirm tote receipt from an AGV
steps.purpose: Establish the first documented action in the normal tipper sequence.
steps.instruction: Confirm from observation or from the source description that the tipper receives a tote from an AGV.
steps.expected_result: The tote is identified as arriving to the tipper from an AGV, matching the documented starting point of the sequence.
steps.healthy_condition: The sequence begins with tote receipt by the tipper from an AGV.
steps.failure_condition: The observed or described sequence does not begin with the tipper receiving a tote from an AGV.

actions.label: Verify AGV-to-tipper receipt
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the tipper receives a tote from an AGV as described in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall operator station context showing the tipper and chute area.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed sequence does not match the documented order of receive, sense, pull up, and empty into the chute.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The tippers receive the totes from the AGVs.

steps.title: Identify the gripper as the sensing component
steps.purpose: Confirm which tipper component performs tote sensing in the documented sequence.
steps.instruction: Observe that the gripper is the component on the tipper that senses the tote. Use Figure 3-4 to identify the gripper if needed.
steps.expected_result: The gripper is identified as the tote-sensing component on the tipper.
steps.healthy_condition: The gripper can be located and recognized as the component referenced in the source description.
steps.failure_condition: The gripper cannot be identified or the sensing component does not match the source description.

actions.label: Identify gripper
actions.exact_action: Observe that the gripper is the component that senses the tote.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Locate the gripper identified in Figure 3-4 as component 14 on the tipper assembly.

stop_or_escalate_if: The gripper cannot be identified from the provided figure or observation.
The observed sensing component does not match the documented gripper reference.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When the gripper on the tipper senses the tote...
Figure 3-4 identifies the gripper as component 14.

steps.title: Verify the gripper pulls the tote up the tipper
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented motion that follows tote sensing.
steps.instruction: Verify that after sensing the tote, the gripper pulls the tote up the tipper.
steps.expected_result: The tote is pulled upward on the tipper after the gripper senses it.
steps.healthy_condition: The sequence proceeds from sensing to pulling the tote up the tipper.
steps.failure_condition: The tote is not pulled up the tipper after sensing, or the order differs from the source description.

actions.label: Verify pull-up motion
actions.exact_action: Verify that after sensing the tote, the gripper pulls the tote up the tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the tipper component diagram as a reference for the gripper and tipper assembly during the pull-up portion of the sequence.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote is not pulled up the tipper after sensing.
The observed order differs from the documented sequence.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When the gripper on the tipper senses the tote, it pulls the tote up the tipper...

steps.title: Verify parcels empty into the chute
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented end state of the normal tipper handling sequence.
steps.instruction: Verify that the tote is tipped so the parcels empty into the chute.
steps.expected_result: Parcels are discharged from the tote into the chute.
steps.healthy_condition: The sequence ends with parcels emptied into the chute.
steps.failure_condition: Parcels do not empty into the chute, or the discharge step does not follow the documented sequence.

actions.label: Verify chute discharge
actions.exact_action: Verify that the tote is tipped so the parcels empty into the chute.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference the chute location in the operator station layout.
Reference the tipper assembly involved in lifting and tipping the tote.

stop_or_escalate_if: Parcels do not empty into the chute.
The observed discharge step does not follow the documented receive, sense, pull-up, and empty order.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: ...and empties the parcels into a chute.

steps.title: Record that the observed sequence matches the documented operating concept
steps.purpose: Close the verification by confirming the observed sequence aligns with the source-described order.
steps.instruction: Record that the observed sequence matches the documented tipper operating concept if tote receipt, gripper sensing, pull-up, and parcel discharge into the chute all occur in order.
steps.expected_result: A documented confirmation exists that the observed sequence matches the manual’s operating description.
steps.healthy_condition: All source-described actions occur in the documented order.
steps.failure_condition: One or more actions are missing, out of order, or inconsistent with the source description.

actions.label: Confirm documented sequence match
actions.exact_action: Record that the observed sequence matches the documented tipper operating concept if all source-described actions occur in order.

stop_or_escalate_if: The observed sequence does not match the documented order of receive, sense, pull up, and empty into the chute.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The tippers receive the totes from the AGVs. When the gripper on the tipper senses the tote, it pulls the tote up the tipper and empties the parcels into a chute.

success_criteria: The user can describe and verify the documented normal tipper handling sequence from tote receipt through parcel discharge.
The sequence is confirmed in the documented order: receive tote from AGV, gripper senses tote, gripper pulls tote up the tipper, and parcels empty into the chute.
healthy_conditions: The tipper receives a tote from an AGV.
The gripper is identified as the tote-sensing component.
After sensing, the gripper pulls the tote up the tipper.
All actions occur in the documented order.
failure_conditions: The observed sequence does not match the documented order of receive, sense, pull up, and empty into the chute.
The gripper cannot be identified as the sensing component from the provided source evidence.
The source-supported sequence cannot be confirmed from observation or documentation.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed sequence does not match the documented order of receive, sense, pull up, and empty into the chute.
Do not add recovery or troubleshooting actions because the source section provides operating description only.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Gripper identification and tipper assembly reference for the documented sequence.
Operator station context including tipper and chute area.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The tippers receive the totes from the AGVs. When the gripper on the tipper senses the tote, it pulls the tote up the tipper and empties the parcels into a chute.
Figure 3-4 identifies the gripper as component 14.

missing_details: The source provides a high-level operating description only and does not provide detailed operator controls or execution steps.
The source does not provide commands, HMI actions, timing, acceptance thresholds, or quantitative validation criteria.
The source does not specify role boundaries beyond general operator use.
The source does not provide explicit production stop or LOTO requirements for this explanatory sequence.
